pi_items.source: Verified in pom.xml (groupId:artifactId and file structure) and Eclipse environment data directory
Verified in setup_task.sh which copies from /workspace/data/inventory-service to /home/ga/inventory-service and export_result.sh which inspects PROJECT_DIR=/home/ga/inventory-service

pi_items.metadata_value: StockCounter — plain int fields need AtomicInteger
pi_items.verified_value: StockCounter.java contains three plain int fields (totalProcessed, totalReceived, totalSold) used in non-atomic increment operations recordSale() and recordReceipt()
pi_items.source: Source code inspection of /workspace/data/inventory-service/src/main/java/com/example/inventory/StockCounter.java lines 13-24. Code comments explicitly note: 'BUG: plain int is not thread-safe — use AtomicInteger'

pi_items.verified_value: ProductCatalog.java uses HashMap for both productsBySku and productsById maps (lines 22-23) which are accessed without synchronization in addProduct, findBySku, findById, getAllProducts, size, and remove methods
pi_items.source: Source code inspection of /workspace/data/inventory-service/src/main/java/com/example/inventory/ProductCatalog.java. Code comment (line 14-17) states: 'BUG: Uses HashMap which is not thread-safe. Concurrent reads and writes can cause HashMap internal state corruption'

pi_items.metadata_value: InventoryManager — HashMap + non-atomic check-then-act in removeStock()
pi_items.verified_value: InventoryManager.java uses HashMap for stockLevels (line 22) AND removeStock() method (lines 37-45) contains classic check-then-act race condition: reads with getOrDefault (line 38), checks condition (line 39), then writes with put (line 43) as separate non-atomic operations
pi_items.source: Source code inspection of /workspace/data/inventory-service/src/main/java/com/example/inventory/InventoryManager.java. Code comments explicitly identify both issues: 'BUG: HashMap is not thread-safe' (line 21) and 'BUG: check-then-act is not atomic — race condition allows overdraft' (lines 35-36). The removeStock implementation demonstrates the vulnerability at lines 38-43

pi_items.metadata_value: ReservationService — unsynchronized compound operations on two maps
pi_items.verified_value: ReservationService.java contains two HashMaps (reservedQuantities and activeReservationIds, lines 22-23) with unsynchronized compound operations. createReservation() (lines 34-46) performs multiple non-atomic steps across two maps. cancelReservation() (lines 52-63) has check-then-remove race condition on activeReservationIds at lines 53 and 58
pi_items.source: Source code inspection of /workspace/data/inventory-service/src/main/java/com/example/inventory/ReservationService.java. Code comments state: 'BUG: Multiple compound operations on shared state are not synchronized' (lines 12-17). Both methods demonstrate the vulnerability: createReservation has concurrent double-booking risk (line 38 comment notes another thread can pass hasStock check simultaneously), cancelReservation has concurrent cancellation risk (line 56 comment)
Verified in pom.xml properties: maven.compiler.source and maven.compiler.target both set to 17. Also confirmed in install_eclipse.sh which installs openjdk-17-jdk and openjdk-17-jre
Verified in pom.xml property junit.version=5.10.0 and dependency org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter with version ${junit.version}

Verified through pom.xml presence, export_result.sh which runs 'mvn clean test', setup_task.sh references task_utils.sh which contains run_maven function, and install_eclipse.sh installs maven package
Verified by reading /workspace/data/inventory-service/src/test/java/ which contains one test file: InventoryManagerTest.java

privileged_info_summary: The inventory-service project is a synthetic educational Java application demonstrating concurrent programming bugs. The project uses Java 17 and JUnit 5.10.0 with Maven for building. The codebase contains exactly four documented thread-safety violations: (1) StockCounter uses plain int fields instead of AtomicInteger, causing lost updates under concurrent increment; (2) ProductCatalog uses non-thread-safe HashMap for product lookups; (3) InventoryManager combines non-thread-safe HashMap usage with a check-then-act race condition in removeStock() that allows stock to go negative; (4) ReservationService performs unsynchronized compound operations across two maps (reservedQuantities and activeReservationIds), enabling double-booking and cancellation races. The project contains one existing JUnit 5 test file (InventoryManagerTest.java) with single-threaded tests. All metadata claims in task.json accurately describe the bugs present in the source code.
